rfi_text: The following items are missing from your application and are required to demonstrate compliance with the Building Code: [list]. The 20 working day processing period is now suspended until the requested information is received. Please supply the requested information so processing can resume.
expected_response: Submission of all missing items.
source: WDC Let's Get it Right Step 5

rfi_text: The plans show specific engineering design (e.g. foundations, retaining walls, structural steel) but do not include sufficient detail to demonstrate Building Code compliance. Per WDC's published process, plans must show compliance via one of: an acceptable solution / verification method, a determination, a current national multi-use approval, a current product certificate, or an alternative solution. Please identify the compliance pathway and provide supporting documentation.
expected_response: Identified compliance pathway + supporting calculations/drawings/PS1.
source: WDC Let's Get it Right Step 5

rfi_text: The application indicates that a Producer Statement for Construction (PS3) will be relied upon as supporting evidence for [scope of work]. Please confirm: (a) the PS3 author, (b) their professional registration, (c) the scope of work covered, and (d) when the PS3 will be supplied. Note: WDC retains discretion under the Building Act on whether to rely on a Producer Statement.
expected_response: Letter from PS3 author confirming scope, registration, and delivery timeline.
source: WDC Let's Get it Right Step 4 (Producer Statement) + Glossary

rfi_text: This commercial building includes specified systems requiring a Compliance Schedule under the Building Act 2004. Please provide draft Compliance Schedule content with performance standards, inspection procedures, maintenance procedures, and reporting procedures for each safety feature.
expected_response: Draft Compliance Schedule covering all specified systems with full IM&R procedures and performance standards.
source: WDC Let's Get it Right Step 3 + Step 5 + Glossary

rfi_text: WDC has assessed that the proposed building work materially affects District Plan / Regional Plan compliance. A section 37 notice is being issued. Building work cannot proceed until a Resource Consent is obtained. Building consent processing is suspended pending resolution.
expected_response: Either (a) Resource Consent decision document, or (b) revised plans removing the s37 trigger.
source: WDC Let's Get it Right Step 2 §s37_notice

rfi_text: This application has been allocated to validation by a Senior Building Officer (Step 6 QA gate). On QA review, the documentation does not accurately represent how compliance will be achieved with respect to [item]. Please clarify the inconsistency.
expected_response: Updated documentation reconciling the inconsistency identified.
source: WDC Let's Get it Right Step 6

rfi_text: The Code Compliance Certificate Auditor has reviewed all inspections, related documentation, and on-site photos. Outstanding items have been identified: [list]. A Code Compliance Certificate cannot be issued until these are resolved. Please supply the requested information or carry out additional works.
expected_response: Outstanding items supplied; further inspection booked if works required.
source: WDC Let's Get it Right Step 17

rfi_text: Form 6 (Code Compliance Certificate application) has been submitted but the following supporting documents required per WDC's CCC checklist are missing: [PS3 Wet Area Seal-Tanking / PS3 Septic Tank and Effluent Disposal field / PS3 Solid Fuel Burner installer / Form 6A Memorandum of Records of Works / LBP Record of Works / Engineer's Site Notes + PS4 / Wet Area Tanking PS / Roofing Membrane PS + warranty / Electrical CoC + Safety Cert / Plumbers Pressure Test / Gas Certificate]. Please supply.
expected_response: All applicable producer statements, certificates, and records as listed in the WDC CCC Checklist Fact Sheet.
source: WDC Code Compliance Certificate Checklist Fact Sheet (QD-BU-Information-004)

rfi_text: This building uses a private well as the sole means of domestic water supply. A second water test is required for CCC. As the time elapsed between the original test (for consent) and now exceeds 18 months, OR the original test was non-compliant, a full water test (chemical AND microbiological) is required — not just microbiological. Please supply per WDC test parameters and the Drinking Water Standards for NZ 2005 (Revised 2018).
expected_response: Full chemical + microbiological water test results from a recognised laboratory, complying with WDC test suite + DWSNZ 2005 (rev 2018).
source: WDC Code Compliance Certificate Checklist + Building on a Rural Site fact sheets

rfi_text: This rural application proposes an on-site effluent system. The application does not include site-specific effluent field design with required fencing. Please provide: (a) site-specific effluent field design from a drainage consultant, (b) fencing details for the effluent field area, (c) compliance with the Canterbury Land and Water Regional Plan, (d) ECan approval if applicable.
expected_response: Effluent field design + fencing detail + ECan approval (if applicable).
source: WDC Building on a Rural Site §effluent_fields

rfi_text: The site soil permeability does not support a soak pit for stormwater disposal. Please provide site-specific stormwater design including swales, ponds, or other appropriately-engineered systems.
expected_response: Site-specific stormwater design with calculations + percolation test results.
source: WDC Building on a Rural Site §stormwater

rfi_text: The proposed earthworks around the dwelling do not maintain a secondary flow path for water run-off. Please revise the site plan/contour drawings to demonstrate that water run-off has a clear secondary flow path that does not threaten the building.
expected_response: Revised site contour drawings showing secondary flow path.
source: WDC Building on a Rural Site §earthworks

rfi_text: The Finished Floor Level (FFL) has not been designed for potential flooding hazards or freeboard requirements of the site. Please provide a flood-hazard assessment and a designed FFL with freeboard.
expected_response: Flood-hazard assessment + FFL design with freeboard.
source: WDC Building on a Rural Site §floor_level

rfi_text: The application labels rooms as 'study' or 'office' but the effluent disposal calculation does not appear to account for these as potential bedrooms. Per WDC's published guidance, rooms that could potentially be used as bedrooms (study/office) are considered as bedrooms for the purposes of effluent field design. Please revise the effluent volume calculation accordingly.
expected_response: Revised effluent volume calc treating study/office as bedrooms; revised effluent field if necessary.
source: WDC Building on a Rural Site §bedrooms_for_effluent_calc

rfi_text: This application does not waive the Building Location Certificate (BLC) requirement. Please provide a BLC from a Registered Professional Surveyor or Licensed Cadastral Surveyor for FFL, recession plane, and boundary setback verification. Reference NZVD2016 or LVD1937 as appropriate per District Plan / Resource Consent. Two established benchmarks must be used (one to set out levels, second to close the circuit).
expected_response: BLC from RPSurv or LCS with two benchmark references and correct vertical datum.
source: WDC Building Location Certificate Fact Sheet

rfi_text: The Site and Level Plan FFL is set out from features (e.g. manhole lid, sump grate) that are not specific subdivision benchmarks or LINZ 1st/2nd order vertical geodetic marks. To waive the BLC requirement, FFL must be set out from a verified benchmark. Please update the survey reference, or supply a BLC.
expected_response: Updated Site and Level Plan with FFL set out from verified subdivision benchmark or LINZ mark, OR BLC from registered surveyor.
source: WDC Building Location Certificate Fact Sheet §waiver_conditions

rfi_text: The application proposes converting an existing building (e.g. shipping container, garage, farm shed) to a habitable use without a Change of Use notice. Per the Building Act 2004, owners must give written notice of a change of use, and Council must approve before the project commences. Please submit a formal Change of Use notice. The new use must comply with: means of escape from fire, protection of other property, sanitary facilities, structural performance, fire rating, accessibility, and continue to comply with other Code provisions to at least the same extent as before.
expected_response: Change of Use notice + supporting compliance demonstration for each triggered Code clause.
source: WDC Building Change of Use Fact Sheet

rfi_text: The proposed change of use creates a new household unit where one did not previously exist. Per the Building Act 2004, the building must comply 'as nearly as is reasonably practicable' with the entire Building Code. Please provide a Code clause-by-clause compliance schedule demonstrating ANARP compliance.
expected_response: Full Code-clause ANARP schedule signed by lead designer.
source: WDC Building Change of Use Fact Sheet §household_unit_creation

rfi_text: This change of use to habitable accommodation requires demonstration of compliance with means of escape from fire (Clause C/AS1 or equivalent) and fire rating of construction. The current application does not address either. Please provide a fire engineering report or AS1 compliance pathway.
expected_response: Fire engineering report or C/AS1 compliance pathway documentation.
source: WDC Building Change of Use Fact Sheet §change_of_use_compliance

rfi_text: This pool installation does not show a backflow preventer. WDC requires a backflow preventer to ensure pool water cannot flow back into the water supply and contaminate it. Pools must also comply with Building (Pools) Amendment Act 2016 (Subpart 7A of the Building Act) and F9. Please update the design to include backflow preventer and confirm F9 compliance for barriers, doors, and windows opening into the pool area.
expected_response: Updated plumbing design with backflow preventer + F9 barrier/access compliance demonstration.
source: WDC Building Inspections Fact Sheet §swimming_pool

rfi_text: The application does not include a buildable truss layout, bracing calculations, or fixing details. These are required to demonstrate compliance with B1 - Structure. Please provide.
expected_response: Truss layout drawings + bracing calculations + truss/rafter fixing details.
source: WDC Building Inspections + standard B1 requirements

rfi_text: The specification references a CodeMark-certified product, but the certificate of conformity has not been provided, nor has documentation showing compliance with the conditions and limitations of use. Please provide the CodeMark certificate and a statement showing how the proposed use meets the certificate's conditions.
expected_response: CodeMark certificate + applicability statement.
source: WDC Let's Get it Right Step 5 (current product certificate compliance pathway)

rfi_text: This application form is incomplete or incorrectly filled in. Per WDC's published process, applications with incomplete information cause the application to be rejected and require re-application with complete/correct information. Please review and resubmit a complete Form 2 with all supporting documentation.
expected_response: Complete Form 2 + all supporting documents per WDC's required submission list.
source: WDC Let's Get it Right Step 4

rfi_text: For drainage inspections, WDC requires an as-built drainage plan. The submitted documentation does not include this. Please commit to providing an as-built drainage plan at the drainage inspection stage.
expected_response: Confirmation in inspection schedule that as-built drainage plan will be supplied.
source: WDC Building Inspections Fact Sheet §drainage

rfi_text: The proposed solid fuel burner installation may require Environment Canterbury approval under the National Emission Standard. WDC notes that on rural sites over 2 hectares most types of clean-air approved burners are permitted, but please confirm with ECan that your specific site permits the proposed burner.
expected_response: ECan confirmation that the specific burner is permitted on the specific site.
source: WDC Building on a Rural Site §solid_fuel_burners

rfi_text: This application appears to relate to building work already completed without consent. WDC cannot issue a building consent retrospectively. You may apply for a Certificate of Acceptance (Form 8) under section 96 of the Building Act 2004. Note that COAs cannot be issued for work pre-1 July 1992, for Schedule 1 exempt work, and Council is under no obligation to issue. A Compliance Officer inspection will be required, with invasive areas left exposed.
expected_response: Form 8 COA application + supporting evidence; inspection access arranged.
source: WDC Certificate of Acceptance Fact Sheet

rfi_text: The application includes plumbing/gas/electrical work that requires Energy Work Certificates at completion. Please confirm in the inspection schedule that licensed plumber/gasfitter/electrician will provide Energy Work Certificates as part of the CCC documentation.
expected_response: Inspection schedule annotated with Energy Work Certificate provision commitment.
source: WDC Let's Get it Right Step 16 + Glossary

rfi_text: The submitted retaining wall design exceeds the limits of B1/AS1 (commonly 1.5m height for residential, 3.0m rural with CPEng). Specific engineering design is required. Please provide PS1 + calculations + drawings from a registered structural engineer.
expected_response: SED retaining wall design with PS1 + calculations + drawings.
source: SDC Building Consent Requirements §retaining_walls (equivalent at WDC) + B1/AS1
